title: Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1991 video game)

'''''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back''''' is a video game released for the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Entertainment System|Nintendo Entertainment System]] in [[1991]], following a similar game based on the original ''[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Star Wars]]'' film.

This is the second of three video games based on ''[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|The Empire Strikes Back]]'' for home video game systems. It was preceded by a [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1982 video game)|version]] for the [[Wikipedia:Atari 2600|Atari 2600]] and succeeded by ''[[Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' for the [[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|SNES]].

A "port," or conversion, of this game was released for the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Game Boy|Nintendo Game Boy]]. That product was reprinted and distributed by several publishers over the course of three years.

==Gameplay==
The object is to destroy an Imperial [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Probe Droid]], escape a [[Wampa/Legends|Wampa]]-infested ice cavern, fight during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], meet Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] on Dagobah to train with him, and attempt to rescue your friends in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] from [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Playing as [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], you can fight with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] or a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Players can ride a [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]]. You can also board a [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeder]] during the Battle of Hoth.

*[[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]]
*[[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] Swamp Creatures
*Imperial [[Cold assault stormtrooper|Snowtroopers]]
*[[Viper probe droid/Legends|Probots]]
*[[Wampa/Legends|Wampas]]

==Legacy==
{{Quote|Time will come for... the return of the Jedi.|the game's closing text|<ref name="The Empire Strikes Back (video game)">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (1991 video game)|''The Empire Strikes Back (video game)'']]</ref>}}
As the ''Empire'' game was released towards the end of the NES's lifecycle, a corresponding game based on [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] was never developed or released, despite being hinted at in the text at the end of the game. ''[[Super Star Wars]]'' for the [[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo Entertainment System]] was released nearly one year later in [[1992]].
